original_abstract: Practical activities are essential methodological tools in Science education, fostering critical thinking and student engagement with the natural world. Despite their importance, theoretical lectures often dominate the classroom. This study sought to evaluate, characterize, and promote the use of practical activities in two public schools in Vitória de Santo Antão, PE—one located in a rural area (Duque de Caxias) and the other in an urban setting (3 de Agosto). Data collection involved site visits and questionnaires administered to Science teachers. The findings indicated that the rural school (Duque de Caxias) possessed better infrastructure and demonstrated a higher teacher commitment to practical experimentation compared to the urban school. Educators from both institutions identified several barriers to implementing these activities, most notably the lack of physical resources and materials, overcrowded classrooms, and severe time constraints. To address these challenges and support the teaching-learning process, the researchers developed instructional workbooks covering content from the 6th to 9th grades of elementary school, providing educators and students with accessible strategies for integrating practical science in the classroom.
humanized_abstract: When talking about the teaching and learning process of Science, the practical activities stand out as a tool in the teacher’s pedagogical arsenal for developing the student’s critical character and increasing their interaction with nature. On the other hand, during the Science classes, the practical activities are frequently replaced by theoretical teaching. In this context, this work has the objective of evaluating, characterizing and proposing the implementation of practical activities in two public schools of Vitória de Santo Antão (PE) located in the country side, Duque de Caxias school, and in the city, 3 de Agosto school. The methodology consisted of interviews with the Science teachers, as well as site visits. After an analytical process of the collected information it was verified that the Duque de Caxias school is an area more prepared to the realization of experimental activities, due to the disposition of the teachers and their focus on hands-on activities, whereas 3 de Agosto school, situated in an urban area, as a common characteristic with the Duque de Caxias school, faced the same obstacles, that, as described by the teachers, consisted of lack of materials and space in the classrooms, as well as high number of students, and a insufficient number of hours to prepare each class. In order to reduce these negative factors and, therefore, improve the teaching and learning process carried out by the teachers in their respective classes, the proposed activities consist of workbooks, structured in modules that relate the theoretical aspects with the corresponding practical activities that have to be carried out by the students at the time of the Science class, enabling them to put into practice the concepts that they studied in the theoretical stage. The contents have as objective to complement the teaching performed by the teachers in the 6th to 9th grade levels.
